name: Solar System
description: Planets, moons, the Sun, and space exploration

questions.q: Since 2006, how many recognized planets are there in the Solar System?

source.notes: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet by the IAU in 2006.

questions.q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?

source.notes: About 58 million km from the Sun on average.

questions.q: What is the largest planet in our Solar System?

source.notes: About 11 times the diameter of Earth.

questions.q: Which planet is often called the 'Red Planet'?

source.notes: Reddish hue is caused by iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.q: Which planet is instantly recognizable for its prominent system of icy rings?

source.notes: All four gas giants have rings, but Saturn's are by far the most visible.

questions.q: What is the name of Earth's only natural satellite?

source.notes: About 384,000 km from Earth.

questions.q: The Sun is classified as what kind of astronomical object?

source.notes: A yellow dwarf (G-type main-sequence) star.

questions.q: How many natural satellites (moons) does Venus have?

source.notes: Along with Mercury, Venus is one of only two planets with no moons.

questions.q: What are the names of the two moons of Mars?

source.notes: Named for the Greek gods of fear and terror.

questions.q: What is the name of the largest moon in the Solar System, orbiting Jupiter?

source.notes: Larger than the planet Mercury.

questions.q: Which planet has the hottest surface temperatures in the Solar System, despite not being the closest to the Sun?

source.notes: Thick CO2 atmosphere traps heat; surface around 465°C.

questions.q: Sunlight takes approximately how long to travel from the Sun to the Earth?

source.notes: About 8 minutes and 20 seconds.

questions.q: Which element makes up about 92% of the Sun's composition?

source.notes: Hydrogen is fused into helium in the Sun's core.

questions.q: How many Earth years does Uranus take to complete one orbit around the Sun?

source.notes: Uranus is about 19 AU from the Sun.

questions.q: Approximately one day on Venus (one full rotation) takes how long in Earth terms?

source.notes: Venus rotates retrograde; a Venusian day is longer than its year.

questions.q: The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into low Earth orbit in which year?

source.notes: Aboard Space Shuttle Discovery, mission STS-31.

questions.q: The Cassini–Huygens probe, a joint NASA/ESA mission, studied which planet and its moons from 2004 to 2017?

source.notes: Ended with a deliberate plunge into Saturn's atmosphere on September 15, 2017.

questions.q: What is the name of NASA's mission that made the first-ever flyby of Pluto in July 2015?

source.notes: Launched 2006; still traveling through the Kuiper Belt.

questions.q: Triton, the largest moon of Neptune, is unusual for what reason?

source.notes: Retrograde orbit suggests it was captured from the Kuiper Belt.

questions.q: What is the name of the largest moon of Uranus?

source.notes: Named after the fairy queen in Shakespeare's 'A Midsummer Night's Dream'.

questions.q: The first spacecraft to successfully land on Mars and transmit data, Viking 1, did so in which year?

source.notes: Landed July 20, 1976 — exactly seven years after the first Moon landing.

questions.q: The Sun accounts for roughly what percentage of the Solar System's total mass?

source.notes: Contains about 99.8% of the Solar System's total mass.
